titleStmt.title: Letter from Charlie Daly to Kate Allman, 6 November 1922
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Charlie Daly

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

correspAction.placeName: Drumboe Castle, Stranorlar, Tirconail, Donegal, Ireland
Providence Convent, Wood Green, London, England

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Miss K Allman
Providence Convent
Wood Green
London 6th Nov 22
Dromboe Castle
Stranorlar
Tirconaill Nov 6th 22 A Chatharna You see by above add. that
Liberty & I have once more parted company
but like on previous occasions let's hope
not for long. For once Luck was against me &
I was had by a mere fluke. The wonder of
the thing is that it did not happen long ago.
However, in a life like this we must take
such incidents as philosophically as possible. In case you have written to the last add.
I gave you you had better recall your letter
or, if you like, write & ask them to post it to
me here. After all I may be in, or
around Dublin when you are passing through.
Miccal may be have influence enough to obtain
you a visit. Iny Anyway. Send me over
some newspapers & books. Sorry that I was
not outside until I heard from you again Beannacht leat, Do chara Cormach Mac D
